paper_quote: 26  tan(x)    K=5    sin x / cos x
informal_pl: tan x = sin x / cos x; warunek cos x ≠ 0.
informal_en: tan x = sin x / cos x; needs cos x ≠ 0.
lean_target_signature: theorem emlterm1c_for_tan : ∃ t : EMLTermℂ₁, ∀ x : ℝ, Real.cos x ≠ 0 → (EMLTermℂ₁.eval (x : ℂ) t).re = Real.tan x := by sorry
notes: Round 2 chunk; Table S2 step 26. | PARTIAL — STRUCTURAL BLOCKER (verified 2026-04-27): inherits the impossibility from chunks 062 (cos) and 063 (sin). With purely real input z = (x:ℂ), no EMLTermℂ₁ term can have its real part equal `tan x` because tan is periodic and the achievable Re-functions are exp/log compositions of x with constant imaginary contributions — non-periodic. Aristotle's `evalI` variant (mapping var → x*I) achieves cos and sin in the real and imaginary parts; the pure-`eval`-on-real-input target stays sorry.
